rulings.Case Title: AGATON GUALBERTO ET AL VS. OCWEN LOAN SERVICING LLC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday February 28, 2019 line 5. DEFENDANT IMPAC FUNDING CORPORATION, IMPAC SECURED ASSET CORPORATION DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT sustained without leave to amend. In addition to the reasons provided in the ruling on co-defendants' demurrer, there is no actual controversy between Plaintiff and IMPAC. Plaintiff does not allege that IMPAC has a current claim to the property and Plaintiff does not allege that IMPAC claims to be a member of MERS. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: AGATON GUALBERTO ET AL VS. OCWEN LOAN SERVICING LLC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday February 28, 2019 line 5. DEFENDANT MORTGAGE ELECTRONIC REGISTRATION SYSTEMS, INC., OCWEN LOAN SERVICING, LLC, DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, AS TRUSTEE DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT sustained without leave to amend as to all causes of action as alleged. Plaintiff attempts to challenge Deutsche Bank's authority/standing to foreclose pre-foreclosure. This challenge is not allowed under California law. Plaintiff's argument that her action is proper because it is a "pre-sale" lawsuit "based on alleged misconduct by the defendants supported by specific facts" fails as a matter of law. Plaintiff allegations in the complaint show that her loan ended up where it was supposed to, with the trustee of IMPAC Secured Assets Trust 2006-6 (See paragraph 21, 22). The original Deed of Trust allowed for the loan to be transferred and designated MERS as a nominee for the Lender and Lender's successors and assigns. Herrera v. Fed. Nat'l Mortgage Ass'n (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 1495, disapproved on other grounds. As the Court has previously allowed, the Plaintiff could have stated a declaratory relief action to determine who Plaintiff is supposed to repay the loan to: the original lender, one of the interim transferees, or Secured Assets Trust 2006-6. Plaintiff chose to not make such amendment and instead is seeking relief prohibited by California law, which includes non-judicial foreclosure scheme. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: OPTIMAL PARTNERS ET AL VS. WILL ROSTOV ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday February 28, 2019 line 8. DEFENDANT WILL ROSTOV DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts showing that Mark Giraudo was a manager of Lincoln and 3rd Partners, LLC on February 9, 2018 or that he was authorized (factually and legally) to represent himself as "manager of Lincoln and 3rd Partners, LLC" as of that date. If Plaintiff is not able to make such allegation in good faith, Plaintiff may in good faith allege facts giving rise to the asserted conclusion that the Rent Board "has determined that the property¿ has been withdrawn." Plaintiff to allege facts showing that the Rent Board had authority to make the determination that the Memorandum was valid, that it was required to make such determination prior to the recordation of the constraints; and, and that it in fact made such a determination. Demurrer in all other grounds is overruled. =(501/REQ)
